ddls:
CREATE TABLE "economic_indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the economic indicator (e.g., GDP Growth Rate, Unemployment Rate) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the economic indicator */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the economic indicator (e.g., percentage, currency) */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the economic indicator (e.g., Macro, Micro) */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the indicator (e.g., quarterly, annually) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update time for the indicator */,
  "data_quality" INTEGER /* Quality rating of the data source (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "code" TEXT /* ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 code for the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the country belongs to (e.g., Asia, Europe) */,
  "currency" TEXT /* Currency of the country */,
  "population" TEXT /* Population of the country */,
  "gdp" TEXT /* GDP of the country */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "population" TEXT /* Population of the region */,
  "area" TEXT /* Area of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source */,
  "contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the contact person at the data source */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the contact person at the data source */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the contact person at the data source */,
  "data_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data updates from the source */,
  "data_quality" INTEGER /* Quality rating of the data source (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_points" (
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "time" TEXT /* Date of the data point */,
  "value" REAL /* Actual value of the data point */,
  "forecast" REAL /* Forecasted value of the data point */,
  "previous" REAL /* Previous value of the data point */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the data point */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Any additional notes or comments about the data point */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES economic_indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_points_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, researcher, portfolio manager) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team of the user */,
  "active_status" INTEGER /* Active status of the user (true/false) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Date and time of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the data */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user accessing the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "indicator_categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each category */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the category */,
  "parent_category_id" INTEGER /* ID of the parent category (for hierarchical categories) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indicator_categories_parent_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_category_id") REFERENCES indicator_categories ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "indicator_units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each unit */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the unit */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the unit */,
  "symbol" TEXT /* Symbol of the unit (e.g., %, USD) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_indicators" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update time for the data source and indicator combination */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id", "indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "country_regions" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last update time for the country and region combination */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id", "region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "indicator_values" (
  "value_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each value */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the economic indicator */,
  "confidence_level" INTEGER /* Confidence level of the value (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("value_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indicator_values_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_indicator_values_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES economic_indicators ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_values" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "forecast" REAL /* Forecasted value of the economic indicator */,
  "confidence_level" INTEGER /* Confidence level of the forecast (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_values_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_values_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES economic_indicators ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "previous_values" (
  "previous_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each previous value */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the economic indicator */,
  "data_point_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point */,
  "previous" REAL /* Previous value of the economic indicator */,
  "confidence_level" INTEGER /* Confidence level of the previous value (1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("previous_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_previous_values_indicator_id FOREIGN KEY ("indicator_id") REFERENCES economic_indicators ("indicator_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_previous_values_data_point_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_point_id") REFERENCES data_points ("data_point_id")
)
